heading: Collocation among United States Postal Service properties
label: 622 Collocation among United States Postal Service properties
notes: Change of NameCommittee on Oversight and Government Reform of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Oversight and Reform of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 6, One Hundred Sixteenth Congress, . Committee on Oversight and Reform of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Oversight and Accountability of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Eighteenth Congress, .Jan. 9, 2019Jan. 9, 2023

children.text: identify a list of postal properties with space available for use by Federal agencies; and

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: .—Voluntary Identification of Postal Property
children.label: b .—Voluntary Identification of Postal Property
children.text: Each year, the Postmaster General may submit the list under subsection (a) to the Council.

children.heading: Submission of List of Postal Properties to Federal Agencies.—
children.label: c Submission of List of Postal Properties to Federal Agencies.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the completion of a list under subsection (a), the Council shall provide the list to each Federal agency.

children.label: B
children.text: review properties under the control of the Federal agency; and

children.heading: .—Terms of Collocation
children.label: d .—Terms of Collocation
children.text: On approval of the recommendations under subsection (c) by the Postmaster General and the applicable agency head, the Federal agency or appropriate landholding entity may work with the Postmaster General to establish appropriate terms of a lease for each postal property.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: e .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section exceeds, modifies, or supplants any other Federal law relating to any competitive bidding process governing the leasing of postal property.
